title: parse_apply_patch fail-closed 'opaque' net is whole-envelope, not per-file — a dropped directive line un-guards a protected write
evidence: parse_apply_patch skips any line inside a recognized envelope that it does not classify as one of the four markers (Add/Delete/Update/Move) — docstring L80-82 and the trailing `# ... skipped` at L143. iter_file_ops only substitutes the fail-closed 'opaque' op when the parse yields ZERO ops (L232-239: `if ops: return ops` else opaque). So a patch that produces >=1 recognized op while silently dropping an unrecognized `*** `-directive line returns the PARTIAL op list and never trips the opaque backstop. Observed (executed parse_apply_patch): input `*** Begin Patch / *** Add File: ok.txt / +hi / *** Copy File: <.codearbiter/CONTEXT.md> / *** End Patch` yields ONLY `write ok.txt` — the Copy-File directive is dropped, ops is non-empty, no opaque op emitted. Same shape observed for a mis-spaced `*** Move to:<path>` (no trailing space): yields `edit a.txt` and drops the rename destination entirely.
impact: IF any supported Codex apply-patch version (min rust-v0.134.0; verification was only against 0.143.0) recognizes a `*** `-directive spelling this Python mirror does not, and applies a write to a protected path, pre-write.py's H-18/H-19/H-05/H-11 guards never see that op and the whole-envelope opaque backstop cannot catch it — an un-guarded write to .codearbiter/CONTEXT.md (disable arbiter), .markers/security-gate-passed (forge a gate pass), or an append-only audit log. The completeness guarantee rests entirely on byte-for-byte parser equivalence across Codex versions with no runtime cross-check.
recommendation: Make the fail-closed net per-file, not whole-envelope: inside a recognized envelope, any line beginning with the `*** ` directive sigil that is NOT one of the four handled markers (and not content/hunk syntax) should force an 'opaque' op (fail CLOSED) rather than being silently skipped. Alternatively assert that the count of `*** `-directive lines equals the number of ops produced and emit opaque on mismatch. Pin and periodically re-verify the mirror against the MINIMUM supported Codex apply-patch parser, not just the latest.
acceptance_criteria: A patch envelope containing an unrecognized `*** `-prefixed directive line alongside a recognized op causes iter_file_ops to return an opaque (fail-closed) op, blocking the whole call
A regression test exercises a partial-parse patch and asserts pre-write BLOCKs (H-21) rather than allowing the recognized-only op subset
